attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- figma:figma-code-connect: Creates and maintains Figma Code Connect template files that map Figma components to code snippets. Use when the user mentions Code Connect, Figma component mapping, design-to-code translation, or asks to create/update .figma.ts or .figma.js files.
- figma:figma-create-new-file: **MANDATORY prerequisite** — you MUST invoke this skill BEFORE every `create_new_file` tool call. NEVER call `create_new_file` directly without loading this skill first. Trigger whenever the user wants a new blank Figma file — a new design, FigJam, or Slides file — or when you need a fresh file before calling `use_figma`. Usage — /figma-create-new-file [editorType] [fileName] (e.g. /figma-create-new-file figjam My Whiteboard, /figma-create-new-file slides Q3 Review)
- figma:figma-generate-design: Use this skill alongside figma-use when the task involves translating an application page, view, or multi-section layout into Figma. Triggers: 'write to Figma', 'create in Figma from code', 'push page to Figma', 'take this app/page and build it in Figma', 'create a screen', 'build a landing page in Figma', 'update the Figma screen to match code', 'convert this modal/dialog/drawer/panel to Figma'. This is the preferred workflow skill whenever the user wants to build or update a full page, modal, dialog, drawer, sidebar, panel, or any composed multi-section view in Figma from code or a description. Discovers design system components, variables, and styles from Code Connect files, existing screens, and library search, then imports them and assembles views incrementally section-by-section using design system tokens instead of hardcoded values.
- figma:figma-generate-diagram: MANDATORY prerequisite — load this skill BEFORE every `generate_diagram` tool call. NEVER call `generate_diagram` directly without loading this skill first. Trigger whenever the user asks to create, generate, draw, render, sketch, or build a diagram — flowchart, architecture diagram, sequence diagram, ERD or entity-relationship diagram, state diagram or state machine, gantt chart, or timeline. Also trigger when the user mentions Mermaid syntax or wants a system architecture, decision tree, dependency graph, API call flow, auth handshake, schema, or pipeline visualized in FigJam. Routes to type-specific guidance, sets universal Mermaid constraints, and tells you when to use a different diagram type or skip the tool entirely (mindmaps, pie charts, class diagrams, etc.).
- figma:figma-generate-library: Build or update a professional-grade design system in Figma from a codebase. Use when the user wants to create variables/tokens, build component libraries, create individual components with proper variant sets and variable bindings, set up theming (light/dark modes), document foundations, or reconcile gaps between code and Figma. Also use when the user asks to create or generate any component in Figma — even a single one — since components require proper variable foundations, variant states, and design token bindings to be production-quality. This skill teaches WHAT to build and in WHAT ORDER — it complements the `figma-use` skill which teaches HOW to call the Plugin API. Both skills should be loaded together.
- figma:figma-swiftui: SwiftUI ↔ Figma translation. Use whenever the user mentions Swift, SwiftUI, iOS, iPhone, or iPad — in EITHER direction — translating a Figma design into SwiftUI (design → code), or pushing SwiftUI views / screens / tokens back into a Figma file (code → design). Triggers on phrases like 'implement this Figma design in SwiftUI', 'build this screen in Swift', 'push this SwiftUI view to Figma', 'mirror my Swift code in a Figma file', or whenever a Figma URL appears alongside `.swift` files / an `.xcodeproj`. Routes to a direction-specific reference doc; loads alongside `figma-use` for the code → design path.
- figma:figma-use: **MANDATORY prerequisite** — you MUST invoke this skill BEFORE every `use_figma` tool call. NEVER call `use_figma` directly without loading this skill first. Skipping it causes common, hard-to-debug failures. Trigger whenever the user wants to perform a write action or a unique read action that requires JavaScript execution in the Figma file context — e.g. create/edit/delete nodes, set up variables or tokens, build components and variants, modify auto-layout or fills, bind variables to properties, or inspect file structure programmatically.
- figma:figma-use-figjam: This skill helps agents use Figma's use_figma MCP tool in the FigJam context. Can be used alongside figma-use which has foundational context for using the use_figma tool.
- figma:figma-use-slides: This skill helps agents use Figma's use_figma MCP tool in the Slides context. Can be used alongside figma-use which has foundational context for using the use_figma tool.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll explore the RTK Query API layer and authentication setup in the edu-pwa admin package. Let me start by understanding the structure.

content.content:
1	import { baseApi } from "@app/store";
2	
3	import type { KtpCatalog, KtpTopicInput } from "@entities/ktp/lib/types";
4	
5	interface ApiResponse<T> {
6	  data: T;
7	}
8	
9	export const {
10	  useGetKtpCatalogQuery,
11	  useUpsertKtpMetaMutation,
12	  useAddKtpTopicMutation,
13	  useUpdateKtpTopicMutation,
14	  useDeleteKtpTopicMutation,
15	  useReorderKtpTopicsMutation,
16	  useImportKtpTopicsMutation,
17	} = baseApi.injectEndpoints({
18	  endpoints: builder => ({
19	    getKtpCatalog: builder.query<KtpCatalog | null, string>({
20	      query: subjectId => `/ktp/${subjectId}`,
21	      transformResponse: (res: ApiResponse<KtpCatalog | null>) => res.data,
22	      providesTags: (_r, _e, subjectId) => [{ type: "Ktp", id: subjectId }],
23	    }),
24	    upsertKtpMeta: builder.mutation<KtpCatalog, { subjectId: string; year?: number; version?: number }>({
25	      query: ({ subjectId, ...body }) => ({ url: `/ktp/${subjectId}`, method: "POST", body }),
26	      transformResponse: (res: ApiResponse<KtpCatalog>) => res.data,
27	      invalidatesTags: (_r, _e, { subjectId }) => [{ type: "Ktp", id: subjectId }],
28	    }),
29	    addKtpTopic: builder.mutation<KtpCatalog, { subjectId: string; body: KtpTopicInput }>({
30	      query: ({ subjectId, body }) => ({ url: `/ktp/${subjectId}/topics`, method: "POST", body }),
31	      transformResponse: (res: ApiResponse<KtpCatalog>) => res.data,
32	      invalidatesTags: (_r, _e, { subjectId }) => [{ type: "Ktp", id: subjectId }],
33	    }),
34	    updateKtpTopic: builder.mutation<
35	      KtpCatalog,
36	      { subjectId: string; topicId: string; body: Partial<KtpTopicInput> }
37	    >({
38	      query: ({ subjectId, topicId, body }) => ({
39	        url: `/ktp/${subjectId}/topics/${topicId}`,
40	        method: "PATCH",
41	        body,
42	      }),
43	      transformResponse: (res: ApiResponse<KtpCatalog>) => res.data,
44	      invalidatesTags: (_r, _e, { subjectId }) => [{ type: "Ktp", id: subjectId }],
45	    }),
46	    deleteKtpTopic: builder.mutation<KtpCatalog, { subjectId: string; topicId: string }>({
47	      query: ({ subjectId, topicId }) => ({
48	        url: `/ktp/${subjectId}/topics/${topicId}`,
49	        method: "DELETE",
50	      }),
51	      transformResponse: (res: ApiResponse<KtpCatalog>) => res.data,
52	      invalidatesTags: (_r, _e, { subjectId }) => [{ type: "Ktp", id: subjectId }],
53	    }),
54	    reorderKtpTopics: builder.mutation<KtpCatalog, { subjectId: string; orderedTopicIds: string[] }>({
55	      query: ({ subjectId, orderedTopicIds }) => ({
56	        url: `/ktp/${subjectId}/reorder`,
57	        method: "POST",
58	        body: { orderedTopicIds },
59	      }),
60	      transformResponse: (res: ApiResponse<KtpCatalog>) => res.data,
61	      invalidatesTags: (_r, _e, { subjectId }) => [{ type: "Ktp", id: subjectId }],
62	    }),
63	    importKtpTopics: builder.mutation<
64	      KtpCatalog,
65	      { subjectId: string; topics: KtpTopicInput[]; year?: number; version?: number; replace?: boolean }
66	    >({
67	      query: ({ subjectId, ...body }) => ({ url: `/ktp/${subjectId}/import`, method: "POST", body }),
68	      transformResponse: (res: ApiResponse<KtpCatalog>) => res.data,
69	      invalidatesTags: (_r, _e, { subjectId }) => [{ type: "Ktp", id: subjectId }],
70	    }),
71	  }),
72	});
73

276	    reorderParagraphs: builder.mutation<
277	      Subject,
278	      { subjectId: string; bookId: string; chapterId: string; topicId: string; orderedParagraphIds: string[] }
279	    >({
280	      query: ({ subjectId, bookId, chapterId, topicId, orderedParagraphIds }) => ({
281	        url: `/subjects/${subjectId}/books/${bookId}/chapters/${chapterId}/topics/${topicId}/paragraphs/reorder`,
282	        method: "POST",
283	        body: { orderedParagraphIds },

285	      transformResponse: (res: ApiResponse<Subject>) => res.data,
286	      invalidatesTags: ["Content"],
287	    }),
288	    getCanonicalRoadmap: builder.query<CanonicalRoadmapResponse, string>({
289	      query: subjectId => ({
290	        url: "/roadmaps/canonical",
291	        params: { subjectId },
292	      }),
293	      transformResponse: (res: ApiResponse<CanonicalRoadmapResponse>) => res.data,
294	      providesTags: (_r, _e, subjectId) => [{ type: "Content", id: `roadmap-${subjectId}` }],
295	    }),
296	    upsertCanonicalRoadmap: builder.mutation<
297	      CanonicalRoadmapResponse,
298	      { subjectId: string; version?: number; nodes: CanonicalRoadmapResponse["nodes"] }
299	    >({
300	      query: body => ({
301	        url: "/roadmaps/admin/canonical",
302	        method: "POST",
303	        body,
304	      }),
305	      transformResponse: (res: ApiResponse<CanonicalRoadmapResponse>) => res.data,
306	      invalidatesTags: ["Content"],
307	    }),
308	    generateCanonicalFromBook: builder.mutation<
309	      CanonicalRoadmapResponse,
310	      { subjectId: string; bookId: string; fullBook?: boolean; chapterId?: string }
311	    >({
312	      query: body => ({
313	        url: "/roadmaps/admin/generate-canonical",
314	        method: "POST",
315	        body,
316	      }),
317	      transformResponse: (res: ApiResponse<CanonicalRoadmapResponse>) => res.data,
318	      invalidatesTags: ["Content"],
319	    }),
320	    updateTopicKtpMapping: builder.mutation<
321	      Subject,
322	      { subjectId: string; bookId: string; chapterId: string; topicId: string; ktpTopicIds: string[] }
323	    >({
324	      query: ({ subjectId, bookId, chapterId, topicId, ktpTopicIds }) => ({
325	        url: `/subjects/${subjectId}/books/${bookId}/chapters/${chapterId}/topics/${topicId}/ktp`,
326	        method: "PUT",
327	        body: { ktpTopicIds },
328	      }),
329	      transformResponse: (res: ApiResponse<Subject>) => res.data,
330	      invalidatesTags: ["Content"],
331	    }),
332	    rebuildRoadmapFromKtp: builder.mutation<
333	      { subjectId: string; version: number; nodesCount: number },
334	      { subjectId: string }
335	    >({
336	      query: body => ({
337	        url: "/roadmaps/admin/rebuild-from-ktp",
338	        method: "POST",
339	        body,
340	      }),
341	      transformResponse: (res: ApiResponse<{ subjectId: string; version: number; nodesCount: number }>) =>
342	        res.data,
343	      invalidatesTags: (_r, _e, { subjectId }) => [{ type: "Content", id: `roadmap-${subjectId}` }],
344	    }),
345	  }),
346	});
347

content.text: I now have everything needed. Let me check the `apiError.ts` helper to document the error-handling convention for mutations.
I have a complete and precise picture. Let me compile the findings.

keyFiles.role: Здесь createStore() из @edu-pwa/core конфигурирует ОДИН baseApi: baseUrl=getApiBase(), credentials:"include" (cookie), getToken (Bearer из adminAuth.token), onUnauthorized -> logout+redirect на /admin/login, reducerPath:"adminApi", tagTypes:["AdminUser","Content","Ktp"]. ВАЖНО: новый tagType (например "Bank") нужно добавить в этот массив — иначе RTK кинет ошибку про неизвестный тег. Экспортирует { baseApi, store }.
ЭТАЛОННЫЙ файл для расширения. Все endpoints добавляются через baseApi.injectEndpoints({ endpoints: builder => ({...}) }) и деструктурируются хуки из результата. Сюда добавляем KC- и bank-эндпоинты.

Типы entity ktp (KtpTopic с _id/order, KtpCatalog, KtpTopicInput). Сюда добавляем KnowledgeComponent/KnowledgeComponentInput/BankCoverage/BankGenerateResult/BankItem и т.п. (адаптация legacy ktp.types.ts).
Public API (barrel) entity. Хуки и типы должны реэкспортироваться отсюда — страницы импортируют ТОЛЬКО из "@entities/ktp", не из ./model/ktpApi напрямую.

createBaseQuery: fetchBaseQuery с prepareHeaders (Authorization: Bearer <token>) + credentials; ловит 401 и зовёт onUnauthorized (кроме skipAuthRedirect). Менять не нужно — cookie уже шлётся через credentials:"include".
ApiResponse<T> = {data,success} и готовый unwrapApiResponse(res)=>res.data. Можно использовать вместо локального ApiResponse как transformResponse.

Второй пример injectEndpoints в тот же baseApi. Показывает паттерн params (getCanonicalRoadmap использует query:()=>({url, params:{subjectId}})) и точечную инвалидацию по id ({type:"Content", id:`roadmap-${subjectId}`}).

Эталон UI-консьюмера: useGetKtpCatalogQuery(subjectId,{skip:!subjectId}), const [addTopic]=useAddKtpTopicMutation(), вызовы через .unwrap() в обёртке run() с busy/error. Новый bank-UI делать по этому шаблону (MUI, AsyncButton, ConfirmDialog).

Точка монтирования панелей через табы (TABS=["ktp","content","mapping","roadmap"], рендер {activeTab==="ktp" && <KtpPanel subjectId={sid}/>}). Сюда добавляется таб/панель банка, ЛИБО банк встраивается внутрь KtpPanel на уровне темы.
Готовые UI-кирпичи: AsyncButton, ConfirmDialog, ConfirmIconButton, EmptyState, PageHeader, apiErrorMessage. Использовать вместо ручных кнопок/диалогов.
apiErrorMessage(e, fallback) — достаёт {data.message} из ошибки .unwrap(). Применять в catch для текста ошибки.

LEGACY референс (axios): точные URL/методы/тела всех KC- и bank-вызовов (propose, confirm{kcIds}, reorder{orderedKcIds}, delete/:kcId, bank/coverage, bank/generate{minPerKc,difficulty}, bank/items?{kcId,status}). Источник истины по контракту.
LEGACY типы KnowledgeComponent/KnowledgeComponentInput/BankCoverage(+BankCoverageKc)/BankGenerateResult/BankItem/QuestionItemStatus — копировать формы в entities/ktp/lib/types.ts.

patterns: ЕДИНЫЙ baseApi, инъекция эндпоинтов. Нет отдельного createApi на фичу — всё инжектится в общий baseApi из @app/store. Файл начинается так: `import { baseApi } from "@app/store";` затем `export const { useXxxQuery, useXxxMutation } = baseApi.injectEndpoints({ endpoints: builder => ({ ... }) });`. Хуки генерятся автоматически из имён эндпоинтов (getFoo -> useGetFooQuery, doFoo (mutation) -> useDoFooMutation).

Распаковка {success,data}. Каждый эндпоинт декларирует `transformResponse: (res: ApiResponse<T>) => res.data`. Локально объявлен `interface ApiResponse<T> { data: T }`. Можно переиспользовать общий `unwrapApiResponse` из @edu-pwa/core. Generic-параметры builder.query<RESULT, ARG> / builder.mutation<RESULT, ARG> описывают УЖЕ распакованный тип.

query как функция, формирующая url/body/params. Query (GET): `query: subjectId => `/ktp/${subjectId}``. Mutation: `query: ({ subjectId, ...body }) => ({ url: `/ktp/${subjectId}`, method: "POST", body })`. Query-параметры: `query: () => ({ url: "/roadmaps/canonical", params: { subjectId } })` (см. getCanonicalRoadmap в subjectApi) — так делать bank/items с {kcId,status}.

Теги. providesTags на query: `providesTags: (_r,_e,subjectId) => [{ type: "Ktp", id: subjectId }]`. invalidatesTags на mutation: `invalidatesTags: (_r,_e,{subjectId}) => [{ type: "Ktp", id: subjectId }]`. tagTypes объявляются ОДИН раз в app/store/index.ts (сейчас ["AdminUser","Content","Ktp"]). Точечная инвалидация по составному id используется в subjectApi: `{ type: "Content", id: `roadmap-${subjectId}` }`.
Сигнатура колбэков тегов: `(_result, _error, arg)`. arg — это второй generic эндпоинта (для KC-мутаций это обычно объект {subjectId, topicId, ...}).

Консьюмер-паттерн (UI). `const { data, isLoading } = useGetKtpCatalogQuery(subjectId, { skip: !subjectId });` и `const [addTopic] = useAddKtpTopicMutation();`. Мутации зовутся через `.unwrap()`: `await addTopic({ subjectId, body }).unwrap();`. Ошибки/загрузка локально через run()-обёртку (try/catch + busy state). MUI + AsyncButton(loading=) + ConfirmDialog/ConfirmIconButton из @widgets/common.

FSD-границы импортов. Внутри entity файлы тянут baseApi через alias `@app/store`, типы через `@entities/ktp/lib/types`. Страницы импортируют хуки/типы ТОЛЬКО из barrel `@entities/ktp` (index.ts реэкспортирует). Алиасы: @app, @entities, @widgets, @pages.

Naming методов под авто-хуки. Чтобы получить ожидаемые имена хуков, эндпоинты называть: getTopicComponents (-> useGetTopicComponentsQuery), proposeTopicComponents, upsertTopicComponent, confirmTopicComponents, reorderTopicComponents, deleteTopicComponent; getBankCoverage, generateBank, getBankItems.
howToImplement: 1) Типы. В /Users/madiever/edu-pwa/packages/admin/src/entities/ktp/lib/types.ts добавить (адаптировав legacy ktp.types.ts): `KnowledgeComponentStatus='proposed'|'confirmed'`; `KnowledgeComponent { _id; title; description?; order; status }`; `KnowledgeComponentInput { id?; title?; description?; order?; status? }`; `BankCoverageKc { kcId; title; active }`; `BankCoverage { totalActive; perKc: BankCoverageKc[]; unassigned }`; `BankGenerateResult { created; rejected; coverage: BankCoverage }`; `QuestionItemStatus='draft'|'active'|'retired'`; `BankItem {...}`. Реэкспортировать новые типы из entities/ktp/index.ts.

2) Tag type. В /Users/madiever/edu-pwa/packages/admin/src/app/store/index.ts расширить tagTypes до ["AdminUser","Content","Ktp","Bank"] (минимум добавить "Bank"; KC можно держать на теге "Ktp" с составным id, но чище ввести и тег KC — тогда добавить и его в массив). Без добавления в этот массив RTK Query упадёт на неизвестном теге.

3) Эндпоинты KC. В ktpApi.ts внутри того же injectEndpoints добавить: getTopicComponents (query GET `/ktp/${subjectId}/topics/${topicId}/components`, transformResponse res.data, providesTags [{type:"Ktp", id:`kc-${subjectId}-${topicId}`}]); proposeTopicComponents (POST .../components/propose, без body); upsertTopicComponent (POST .../components, body=KnowledgeComponentInput; сервер ждёт {id?,title,description?,order?,status?}); confirmTopicComponents (POST .../components/confirm, body {kcIds}); reorderTopicComponents (POST .../components/reorder, body {orderedKcIds}); deleteTopicComponent (DELETE .../components/${kcId}). Все mutation-эндпоинты: `invalidatesTags: (_r,_e,{subjectId,topicId}) => [{ type:"Ktp", id:`kc-${subjectId}-${topicId}` }]` (так list KC рефетчится после propose/upsert/confirm/reorder/delete). Generic результата всех KC-эндпоинтов — KnowledgeComponent[] (сервер возвращает обновлённый список).

4) Эндпоинты банка. Туда же: getBankCoverage (query GET `/ktp/${subjectId}/topics/${topicId}/bank/coverage`, providesTags [{type:"Bank", id:`cov-${subjectId}-${topicId}`}]); getBankItems (query GET .../bank/items, query-параметры kcId/status через `params`, providesTags [{type:"Bank", id:`items-${subjectId}-${topicId}`}]); generateBank (POST .../bank/generate, body {minPerKc?,difficulty?}, результат BankGenerateResult, invalidatesTags обоих Bank-id для (subjectId,topicId)). Поскольку generate меняет покрытие и набор вопросов — инвалидировать и cov-, и items-теги.

5) Хуки. Добавить новые имена в деструктуризацию из injectEndpoints (useGetTopicComponentsQuery, useProposeTopicComponentsMutation, useUpsertTopicComponentMutation, useConfirmTopicComponentsMutation, useReorderTopicComponentsMutation, useDeleteTopicComponentMutation, useGetBankCoverageQuery, useGenerateBankMutation, useGetBankItemsQuery) и реэкспортировать их из entities/ktp/index.ts.

6) UI. Новый компонент по образцу KtpPanel.tsx (MUI Stack/Paper/TextField/Button, AsyncButton, ConfirmIconButton/ConfirmDialog, локальный run()/busy/error, apiErrorMessage в catch). Импорты хуков/типов — из "@entities/ktp". Вызовы мутаций через .unwrap(). Query со skip:!subjectId||!topicId. Разместить либо как блок управления KC + покрытием банка внутри выбранной темы в KtpPanel, либо отдельной панелью, добавив таб в TABS и ветку рендера в SubjectWorkspacePage.tsx (но bank требует topicId — нужен выбор темы, поэтому естественнее встроить на уровень темы).

7) Роли. UI-гейтинг по adminAuthSlice.role (admin vs teacher): teacher видит list/coverage, мутации (propose/upsert/confirm/reorder/delete/generate) и bank/items показывать только при role==='admin'. Сервер всё равно форсит права — это лишь скрытие кнопок.
risks: tagTypes объявлены централизованно в app/store/index.ts — если добавить providesTags/invalidatesTags с типом "Bank" (или новым KC-тегом), но забыть внести его в этот массив, RTK Query выбросит ошибку в dev. Не забыть синхронизировать.

Все KC/bank-эндпоинты привязаны к паре (subjectId, topicId), а не только к subjectId. Тег "Ktp" сейчас используется с id=subjectId для каталога — нельзя инвалидировать им KC, иначе будет лишний рефетч каталога и/или коллизии. Использовать составной id (kc-/cov-/items- + subjectId + topicId).

bank/items и часть KC-мутаций — только admin (cookie+isAdmin на бэке). Запрос items под ролью teacher вернёт 401/403; убедиться, что getBankItemsQuery не вызывается у teacher (skip по role), иначе сработает onUnauthorized -> logout+redirect на /admin/login при 401.

Сервер для confirm/reorder/upsert/delete возвращает ОБНОВЛЁННЫЙ список KC (KnowledgeComponent[]) — можно опираться на возвращаемое значение, но инвалидация list всё равно нужна для коврелирующих view; одновременно generate меняет coverage И items, поэтому одной инвалидации мало — инвалидировать оба Bank-id.

Контракт {id?,title,description?,order?,status?} в upsert приходит как KnowledgeComponentInput, но list/прочие отдают KnowledgeComponent с _id. Не перепутать поля id (вход) и _id (ответ) при маппинге формы.

credentials:"include" + Authorization Bearer одновременно: домен kakoi-to-do-men.ru должен отдавать корректный CORS (Access-Control-Allow-Credentials) — это инфраструктура бэка, на UI не влияет, но при локальной разработке через прокси/CORS cookie может не доходить.

Legacy bankItems поддерживает query-параметры {kcId,status}; задача в промпте описывает GET items без параметров. Реализовать через `params` опционально (RTK сам опускает undefined), чтобы и фильтрация, и простой вызов работали одним эндпоинтом.

content.content: Structured output provided successfully

content.text:
Я изучил RTK Query слой и аутентификацию в `/Users/madiever/edu-pwa/packages/admin` и вернул структурированный отчёт. Кратко главное:

- **Единый baseApi** создаётся через `createStore()` из `@edu-pwa/core` в `app/store/index.ts` (baseUrl=`getApiBase()`, `credentials:"include"` для cookie, Bearer-токен из `adminAuth.token`, 401→logout+redirect, `tagTypes:["AdminUser","Content","Ktp"]`).
- **Все endpoints инжектятся** в этот общий api через `baseApi.injectEndpoints(...)` — отдельного `createApi` на фичу нет. Хуки автогенерируются из имён.
- **Распаковка** `{success,data}` делается на каждом endpoint через `transformResponse: (res) => res.data` (есть и общий `unwrapApiResponse` в core).
- **Теги**: `providesTags`/`invalidatesTags` с составным id; новый tagType (`Bank`) надо добавить в `tagTypes` в `app/store/index.ts`, иначе RTK упадёт.
- **Эталон endpoints** — `entities/ktp/model/ktpApi.ts`; **эталон UI-консьюмера** — `pages/subjectWorkspace/ui/KtpPanel.tsx` (хуки из barrel `@entities/ktp`, мутации через `.unwrap()`, MUI + `AsyncButton`/`ConfirmDialog`, `apiErrorMessage`).
- **Legacy-контракт** точных URL/тел/типов — `GenTestMVP/client/src/api/ktp.api.ts` и `src/types/ktp.types.ts`.

Полный план встраивания KC (list/propose/upsert/confirm/reorder/delete) и банка (coverage/generate/items) с правильными tag-инвалидациями (по составному id `kc-/cov-/items-${subjectId}-${topicId}`) — в полях `howToImplement` и `patterns` структурированного ответа.
